codeReview.summary: Solid two-pronged fix for serial output interleaving during boot. (1) All multi-call sys::print patterns across 5 files consolidated into single-syscall buffer writes, eliminating intra-line byte interleaving from concurrent processes. (2) IPC synchronization added: GPU driver sends MSG_GPU_READY after setup; init waits before spawning compositor. Init also yields 8 times between process starts to stagger startup banners. No kernel serial lock — purely userspace staggering as specified. Buffer sizes are all adequately sized for maximum u32/u64 values (10/20 digits). MSG_GPU_READY = 8 is a clean message type slot (no conflicts). spawn_with_channel was simplified from 6 intermediate prints to single success/failure messages. All changes are consistent with the codebase's no_std, bare-metal patterns.

issues.description: Missing blank line between format_u32's closing brace (line 209) and the next function's doc comment (line 210: '/// Set up the interactive display pipeline with input.'). The rest of the codebase consistently has blank lines between function definitions. Minor style inconsistency.

format_u32 is duplicated identically across 3 files (init, GPU driver, 9p driver). The blk driver has format_u64 (same logic, u64 variant). The compositor uses a pre-existing append_u32 with a slightly different API (returns position vs bytes written). This duplication is understandable since each process is a separate ELF binary in a no_std environment, but if a shared utility library (like sys or ipc) were to gain a format helpers module, all 4 copies should converge.

The yield-based stagger (8 yields between process starts) is a timing heuristic. If a process startup is delayed (e.g., slow 9p mount, future heavier initialization), 8 yields might not be sufficient to prevent inter-line interleaving. However, the primary fix (single-syscall prints) eliminates intra-line garbling regardless, so this is defense-in-depth. Acceptable for a boot sequence in a research project.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The IPC message type numbering scheme (1-7 for config, 8 for GPU_READY, 20 for PRESENT, 40+ for FS) is becoming a convention but isn't documented in AGENTS.md or architecture.md. As more message types are added, a reference table would prevent accidental collisions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/services/init/main.rs:61-69 defines MSG_DEVICE_CONFIG=1 through MSG_GPU_READY=8, then MSG_FS_READ_REQUEST=40. The gaps (9-19, 21-39) are implicit reservations.

summary: Pass. Clean, well-executed fix that addresses the garbled serial output through two complementary mechanisms: (1) single-syscall print consolidation eliminates intra-line byte interleaving across all 5 userspace processes, and (2) IPC synchronization (MSG_GPU_READY) plus yield staggering prevents inter-line interleaving during the boot sequence. The implementation follows the feature's constraint of no kernel serial lock — everything is userspace-only. All 859 tests pass. Verified across 4 QEMU boots by the worker. Three non-blocking issues noted: a missing blank line in init/main.rs, format_u32 code duplication across 3 files, and the yield-count heuristic being timing-dependent (mitigated by the primary single-syscall fix).
